page.title: Boat Rental No License Marbella: Prices and Options

data.meta_description: Boat rental no license Marbella prices: from €749/2h for a 12.5m Astondoa 40. Licensed skipper, drinks & snacks included. Book your no-license charter today.
data.summary: Compare boat rental no license Marbella prices for our fleet of licensed-skipper boats, including the Astondoa 40 from €749/2h and the Azimut 39 from €749/2h. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT – no boating license needed.
key_facts: No license required – licensed skipper included on every charter.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours on a 12.5m boat (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All rates include fuel, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks.
IVA (21% Spanish VAT) included in all prices – no hidden fees.
Fleet departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella, with access to Cabopino and Estepona.
Larger yachts (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94) available from 4 hours with jet ski included.
Maximum capacity ranges from 5 to 12 passengers depending on the boat.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and special requests – no license needed.

faq.q: Do I need a license to rent a boat in Marbella?
faq.a: No, you do not need a boating license for any of our charters. Every boat comes with a licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You simply relax and enjoy the coast.

faq.q: How much does a no-license boat rental in Marbella cost?
faq.a: Our most affordable no-license options start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. For longer trips, the same boats cost €2,299 for 8 hours. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start from €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours.

faq.q: What is included in the rental price?
faq.a: The price includes a professional skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no extra charges.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our boats have coolers and onboard facilities. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the space.

faq.q: How many people can I take on a no-license boat?
faq.a: Capacity varies by boat. The smallest boats (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) take up to 5 passengers, while larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 accommodate up to 12 passengers. Check the specific boat page for exact numbers.

faq.q: Which destinations can we visit without a license?
faq.a: You can explore Puerto Banús, the Marbella coastline, Cabopino, Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and even Estepona or Sotogrande on longer charters. Your skipper will suggest the best route based on weather and your preferences.

faq.q: How do I book a no-license boat rental in Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom requests. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially for weekends and peak season.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety is our priority. If the weather is unsafe for sailing, we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date at no extra cost. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

data.body_html:
Planning a day on the water in Marbella but don't hold a boating license? You can still enjoy a luxury yacht charter without any paperwork. Our boat rental no license Marbella prices start from €749 for 2 hours on a 12.5m Astondoa 40, with a licensed skipper included. Every charter covers fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT – so you just turn up and relax. Whether you want a short spin past the Golden Mile or a full day cruising to Estepona, we have the right boat for you.

No License Required – How It Works

Spanish law requires a license for private boat operation, but our charters bypass that entirely. Every boat in our fleet comes with a professional skipper who handles all navigation, mooring, and safety. You are free to swim, sunbathe, and enjoy the views. The skipper also acts as your local guide, pointing out landmarks like La Concha mountain, Río Verde, and the exclusive villas along the coast. No experience, no exam – just book and go.

Boat Rental No License Marbella Prices 2025

We offer transparent pricing with no surprises. Here are the rates for our most popular no-license charters:

BoatLengthCapacity2 Hours8 Hours
Astondoa 4012.5 m9 pax€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5 m11 pax€749€2,299
Mangusta 8024 m12 pax€4,719 (min 4h)–

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 are available on request – contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. For a full list of options, visit our boat rental Marbella homepage.

Which Boats Can I Rent Without a License?

Every boat in our fleet is available without a license because all charters include a skipper. From the compact 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the impressive 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax), you can choose based on group size and budget. Popular choices for no-license rentals include the Azimut 58 Flybridge for its spacious deck and the Mangusta 80 for its speed and jet ski add-on. Check individual boat pages for exact specs and photos.

What’s Included in the Price?

  • Licensed skipper (handles all navigation)
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Full insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT) – no hidden taxes

You can also bring your own food and drinks. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the onboard facilities.

Best Routes for No-License Charters

From Puerto Banús, your skipper can take you to the iconic Marbella Marina, the quiet coves of Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, or further west to Estepona and Sotogrande. On a 4-hour trip, you can anchor for swimming and lunch near Cabopino. For a full day, head to the pristine waters of the Costa del Sol or even cross to the African coast on longer charters (subject to weather). Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences.

How to Book Your No-License Boat

Booking is simple. Browse our fleet online, choose your preferred boat and duration, then contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form. We recommend booking at least 48 hours ahead, especially in summer. Payment can be made by bank transfer or card. For custom requests – like a specific departure time or catering – just ask. Visit our Marbella boat rental page to get started.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a license to rent a boat in Marbella?

No, you do not need a boating license for any of our charters. Every boat comes with a licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You simply relax and enjoy the coast.

How much does a no-license boat rental in Marbella cost?

Our most affordable no-license options start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. For longer trips, the same boats cost €2,299 for 8 hours. Larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 start from €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours.

What is included in the rental price?

The price includes a professional skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no extra charges.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our boats have coolers and onboard facilities. Just let us know in advance so we can prepare the space.

How many people can I take on a no-license boat?

Capacity varies by boat. The smallest boats (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) take up to 5 passengers, while larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 accommodate up to 12 passengers. Check the specific boat page for exact numbers.

Which destinations can we visit without a license?

You can explore Puerto Banús, the Marbella coastline, Cabopino, Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and even Estepona or Sotogrande on longer charters. Your skipper will suggest the best route based on weather and your preferences.

How do I book a no-license boat rental in Marbella?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom requests. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially for weekends and peak season.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety is our priority. If the weather is unsafe for sailing, we will offer you a full refund or reschedule to another date at no extra cost. The skipper makes the final call on the day.
